RENDER_BLACK.description: Render output is completely black (mean brightness < 10/255)

fix_playbook.step_1: Check light count: if 0, add 3-point lighting (KeySun 2.0, FillSun 1.0, RimSun 1.5)
fix_playbook.step_2: Check world: if None, create world with background strength 1.0
fix_playbook.step_3: Check camera: ensure active camera exists and is not inside any mesh
fix_playbook.step_4: Verify Cycles is enabled and samples >= 64
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
scene = bpy.context.scene
if len(bpy.data.lights) == 0:
    sun_key = bpy.data.lights.new('KeySun', 'SUN')
    sun_key.energy = 2.0
    sun_fill = bpy.data.lights.new('FillSun', 'SUN')
    sun_fill.energy = 1.0
if scene.world is None:
    world = bpy.data.worlds.new('World')
    scene.world = world
scene.world.use_nodes = True
scene.world.node_tree.nodes['Background'].inputs[1].default_value = 1.0
scene.render.engine = 'CYCLES'
scene.cycles.samples = 64

LIGHT_UNDEREXPOSED.description: Image too dark overall (mean brightness 10-80/255), forensic details invisible

fix_playbook.step_1: Measure current mean brightness; if < 80, increase all light energies by 2x
fix_playbook.step_2: Check world background strength; if < 0.8, increase to 1.0
fix_playbook.step_3: In compositor, add Exposure node and increase by 0.5-1.0 stops

LIGHT_OVEREXPOSED.description: Image too bright (mean brightness > 240/255), detail blown out, forensic markers unreadable

fix_playbook.step_1: Reduce all light energies by 50%
fix_playbook.step_2: Reduce world background strength to 0.5-0.7
fix_playbook.step_3: In compositor, add Exposure node and decrease by 0.5-1.0 stops
fix_playbook.step_4: Enable tone mapping in render settings

CAMERA_OCCLUDED.description: Camera view blocked by geometry (expected scene not visible)

fix_playbook.step_1: Move camera 1-2 units away from nearest geometry
fix_playbook.step_2: Reduce camera clip start from 1.0 to 0.1
fix_playbook.step_3: Check camera is not parented to moving geometry
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
camera = bpy.context.scene.camera
if camera:
    camera.data.clip_start = 0.01
    camera.location.z += 2.0
    camera.data.clip_end = 1000.0
    bpy.context.scene.camera = camera

CAMERA_MISFRAMED.description: Primary subject (vehicle, pedestrian) partially or entirely out of frame

fix_playbook.step_1: Adjust camera X/Y position to center primary subject
fix_playbook.step_2: Adjust focal length (24mm for wide, 50mm for medium, 85mm for tight)
fix_playbook.step_3: Verify rule-of-thirds composition: subject at grid intersection
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
camera = bpy.context.scene.camera
subject = bpy.data.objects.get('Vehicle') or bpy.data.objects.get('Pedestrian')
if camera and subject:
    target_x = subject.location.x
    target_y = subject.location.y - 3.0
    camera.location.x = target_x
    camera.location.y = target_y
    camera.data.lens = 50.0

MATERIAL_FLAT.description: Materials have no specular/metallic variation, appear unrealistic flat

fix_playbook.step_1: For vehicle bodies: set Metallic 0.7-1.0, Roughness 0.2-0.4
fix_playbook.step_2: For glass: set Metallic 0.0, Roughness 0.0, IOR 1.45
fix_playbook.step_3: For asphalt: set Metallic 0.0, Roughness 0.8-0.95

MATERIAL_DEFAULT_GREY.description: Objects retain Blender default grey material (255,127,127) unmodified

fix_playbook.step_1: Identify all objects with default grey material
fix_playbook.step_2: For vehicles: assign automotive paint (0.95 base color, 0.3 roughness)
fix_playbook.step_3: For environment: assign appropriate material (asphalt, concrete, grass)

GEOMETRY_PRIMITIVE.description: Scene contains obvious primitive shapes (perfect cubes, spheres, planes) as stand-ins

fix_playbook.step_1: List all objects; identify cubes, spheres, planes with generic names
fix_playbook.step_2: Replace with actual 3D models (import FBX/OBJ of real vehicles/pedestrians)
fix_playbook.step_3: Delete or hide all Cube/Sphere/Plane objects

GEOMETRY_LOW_POLY.description: Primary subject (vehicle, pedestrian) has visibly low polygon count causing jagged edges or missing detail

fix_playbook.step_1: Add Subdivision Surface modifier with 2-3 subdivision levels
fix_playbook.step_2: Import higher-detail model if available
fix_playbook.step_3: If already subdivided, increase levels to 3-4 for hero subject
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
for obj in bpy.data.objects:
    if obj.type == 'MESH' and ('Vehicle' in obj.name or 'Pedestrian' in obj.name or 'Car' in obj.name):
        subsurf = obj.modifiers.new(name='SubSurf', type='SUBSURF')
        subsurf.levels = 3
        subsurf.render_levels = 4
        obj.data.use_smooth = True

MOTION_UNREADABLE.description: Motion blur/velocity vectors not visible; cannot determine speed or direction from animation

fix_playbook.step_1: Enable motion blur in Cycles render settings
fix_playbook.step_2: Increase samples to 128+ for smooth motion
fix_playbook.step_3: Enable Vector pass in Compositing for post-motion enhancement
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
scene = bpy.context.scene
scene.render.engine = 'CYCLES'
scene.cycles.use_motion_blur = True
scene.cycles.motion_blur_position = 'CENTER'
scene.cycles.samples = 128
scene.render.use_motion_blur = True
scene.render.motion_blur_shutter = 0.5

FORENSIC_NO_MARKERS.description: Forensic markers (distance markers, evidence tags, scale references) completely absent

fix_playbook.step_1: Create evidence marker objects at key points (impact zone, sight lines, victim location)
fix_playbook.step_2: Add text labels with case reference and marker numbers
fix_playbook.step_3: Ensure markers visible in viewport with distinct material (bright color)
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
marker_location = (5.0, 0.0, 0.0)
bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_uv_sphere_add(radius=0.3, location=marker_location)
marker = bpy.context.active_object
marker.name = 'ForensicMarker_01'
mat = bpy.data.materials.new(name='MarkerMat')
mat.use_nodes = True
bsdf = mat.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']
bsdf.inputs['Base Color'].default_value = (1.0, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0)
marker.data.materials.append(mat)
print('Created forensic marker at', marker_location)

FORENSIC_NO_SCALE.description: Scale reference (metric ruler, scale bar) missing; impossible to verify dimensions

fix_playbook.step_1: Create scale bar object (1 meter long, 5cm tall) with text label
fix_playbook.step_2: Position in frame foreground or corner, clearly visible and unobstructed
fix_playbook.step_3: Add measurement text: '1 Meter' or '100cm' with case reference
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cube_add(size=1.0, location=(0.0, 0.0, -1.0))
scale_bar = bpy.context.active_object
scale_bar.name = 'ScaleBar_1M'
scale_bar.scale = (1.0, 0.05, 0.05)
mat = bpy.data.materials.new(name='ScaleBarMat')
mat.use_nodes = True
bsdf = mat.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']
bsdf.inputs['Base Color'].default_value = (0.9, 0.9, 0.1, 1.0)
scale_bar.data.materials.append(mat)
print('Created 1-meter scale bar')

FORENSIC_NO_LABELS.description: Case number, exhibit reference, or scene identification labels completely absent

fix_playbook.step_1: Create text object with case number (e.g., 'Case #2024-0521')
fix_playbook.step_2: Create text object with exhibit reference (e.g., 'Exhibit A-3: Impact Zone')
fix_playbook.step_3: Position in frame corner with high contrast (white text, dark background)
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
bpy.ops.object.text_add(location=(0.0, 5.0, 0.0))
text_obj = bpy.context.active_object
text_obj.name = 'CaseLabel'
text_obj.data.body = 'Case #2024-0521\nExhibit A-3'
text_obj.scale = (0.5, 0.5, 0.5)
mat = bpy.data.materials.new(name='TextMat')
mat.use_nodes = True
bsdf = mat.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']
bsdf.inputs['Base Color'].default_value = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0)
text_obj.data.materials.append(mat)
print('Added case label')

COMPOSITION_EMPTY_SPACE.description: Frame contains excessive empty space (> 30% black/empty areas), composition unbalanced

fix_playbook.step_1: Move camera closer to primary subject
fix_playbook.step_2: Reposition camera to follow rule of thirds
fix_playbook.step_3: Add environmental context if needed to fill frame
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
camera = bpy.context.scene.camera
subject = bpy.data.objects.get('Vehicle') or bpy.data.objects.get('Pedestrian')
if camera and subject:
    dist_to_subject = (subject.location - camera.location).length
    new_dist = max(dist_to_subject * 0.7, 3.0)
    direction = (subject.location - camera.location).normalized()
    camera.location = subject.location - (direction * new_dist)
    camera.rotation_euler = (0, 0, 0)

COMPOSITION_NO_DEPTH.description: Scene appears 2D flat; no sense of depth or layering (vehicles appear pasted on)

fix_playbook.step_1: Add foreground elements (trees, poles, buildings) 2-3 units from camera
fix_playbook.step_2: Add background elements (buildings, sky) 50+ units from camera
fix_playbook.step_3: Enable depth of field with f-stop 4-8 to blur background

DEFORMATION_ABSENT.description: Vehicle deformation from collision not visible; impact zone appears undamaged

fix_playbook.step_1: Ensure collision frame shows damaged mesh geometry (fender crumple, hood buckle)
fix_playbook.step_2: If using shape keys, enable damage shape key at frame N (impact frame)
fix_playbook.step_3: Verify collision happens at exact frame; preview timeline to confirm

INTERIOR_MISSING.description: Vehicle interior (dashboard, seats, steering wheel) not visible through windows in close-up shots

fix_playbook.step_1: Model or import vehicle interior geometry (dashboard, seats, headrests)
fix_playbook.step_2: Create interior materials with appropriate colors and details
fix_playbook.step_3: Ensure interior visible through windows in driver POV or side shots

NIGHT_NO_EFFECT.description: Night scene lacks nighttime visual characteristics (too bright, no shadows, no artificial lighting effects)

fix_playbook.step_1: Reduce world background strength to 0.1-0.2 to darken sky
fix_playbook.step_2: Add street lights (point lights with warm color ~2500K) every 20 units along road
fix_playbook.step_3: Add vehicle headlights (spotlights) with forward-facing cones
fix_playbook.step_4: Enable shadow depth in lights and increase shadow samples
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
scene = bpy.context.scene
if scene.world and scene.world.use_nodes:
    bg_node = scene.world.node_tree.nodes.get('Background')
    if bg_node:
        bg_node.inputs[1].default_value = 0.15
for i in range(5):
    light = bpy.data.lights.new(f'StreetLight_{i}', 'POINT')
    light.energy = 150.0
    light.color = (1.0, 0.9, 0.7)
    light_obj = bpy.data.objects.new(f'StreetLight_{i}', light)
    bpy.context.collection.objects.link(light_obj)
    light_obj.location = (i * 20.0, -5.0, 8.0)
scene.cycles.samples = 256

TEMPORAL_FLICKER.description: Rendered animation has temporal flicker (brightness oscillations) frame to frame

fix_playbook.step_1: Disable adaptive sampling; use fixed samples minimum 128
fix_playbook.step_2: Increase seed variance to zero (fixed seed)
fix_playbook.step_3: Verify all light energies are constant (no keyframes on light.energy)

TEMPORAL_DENOISE_SHIMMER.description: Denoising artifact creates shimmer or texture crawl during camera motion or animation

fix_playbook.step_1: Use OptiX or OpenImageDenoise (not OIDN with temporal)
fix_playbook.step_2: Enable temporal consistency in denoiser settings
fix_playbook.step_3: Reduce prefilter intensity if shimmer persists
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
scene = bpy.context.scene
scene.cycles.use_denoising = True
scene.cycles.denoiser = 'OPENIMAGEDENOISE'
scene.render.use_motion_blur = False
scene.cycles.samples = 192
if hasattr(scene.cycles, 'denoising_temporal'):
    scene.cycles.denoising_temporal = True
if hasattr(scene.cycles, 'denoising_temporal_passes'):
    scene.cycles.denoising_temporal_passes = 2

TEMPORAL_EXPOSURE_PUMP.description: Global exposure oscillates frame-to-frame; scene brightens and darkens rhythmically

fix_playbook.step_1: Remove all keyframes from world background strength and light energies
fix_playbook.step_2: In compositor, verify Exposure node has no animation curves
fix_playbook.step_3: Use fixed exposure value (typically 0.0 to 1.0 range)

WITNESS_VISIBILITY_BLOCKED.description: Witness/driver line-of-sight to critical event is obstructed or implausible

fix_playbook.step_1: Reposition camera to location matching witness statement (e.g., driver seat, sidewalk)
fix_playbook.step_2: Verify direct unobstructed line of sight to impact zone
fix_playbook.step_3: Add sight-line visualization (optional: thin line from viewpoint to impact)
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
scene = bpy.context.scene
camera = scene.camera
impact_zone = bpy.data.objects.get('ImpactZone')
if camera and impact_zone:
    direction = (impact_zone.location - camera.location).normalized()
    hit_distance = (impact_zone.location - camera.location).length
    result = bpy.context.scene.ray_cast(bpy.context.view_layer.depsgraph, camera.location, direction)
    if result[0] and result[2] < hit_distance:
        print('WARNING: Sight line is obstructed')
    else:
        print('Sight line is clear')

WEATHER_MISSING.description: Environmental conditions (rain, fog, snow, sun angle) do not match scenario description

fix_playbook.step_1: Adjust sun rotation to match time of day (morning=low angle east, noon=high angle)
fix_playbook.step_2: If rain/wet road stated: add volume scatter and wet material to road
fix_playbook.step_3: If fog stated: add volumetric fog using World Volume Scatter
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
scene = bpy.context.scene
for light in bpy.data.lights:
    if light.type == 'SUN':
        light.angle = 0.05
if scene.world and scene.world.use_nodes:
    links = scene.world.node_tree.links
    volume_scatter = scene.world.node_tree.nodes.new('ShaderNodeVolumeScatter')
    volume_scatter.inputs['Density'].default_value = 0.1
    volume_scatter.inputs['Anisotropy'].default_value = 0.5
    bg_node = scene.world.node_tree.nodes.get('Background')
    if bg_node:
        links.new(volume_scatter.outputs['Volume'], scene.world.node_tree.nodes['World Output'].inputs['Volume'])
print('Added volumetric effects')

LEGAL_DEMO_AID_MISSING.description: DEMONSTRATIVE AID disclaimer or legal notice not present in render

fix_playbook.step_1: Create text object with legal disclaimer: 'DEMONSTRATIVE AID - NOT TO SCALE'
fix_playbook.step_2: Add case reference and scene description
fix_playbook.step_3: Position in frame corner with high contrast and legible font size
fix_playbook.bpy_code:
import bpy
bpy.ops.object.text_add(location=(0.0, -6.0, 0.0))
legal_text = bpy.context.active_object
legal_text.name = 'LegalDisclaimer'
legal_text.data.body = 'DEMONSTRATIVE AID\nNOT TO SCALE'
legal_text.data.size = 0.5
legal_text.scale = (1.5, 1.5, 1.5)
mat = bpy.data.materials.new(name='LegalTextMat')
mat.use_nodes = True
bsdf = mat.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']
bsdf.inputs['Base Color'].default_value = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
legal_text.data.materials.append(mat)
print('Added legal disclaimer')
